title: melia azedarach var. intermedia
intro: Melia azedarach var. intermedia, also known as the Western Chinaberry or Littleleaf Chinaberry, is a deciduous tree native to Australia and Asia, characterized by its small leaves and white or pink flowers. The bark, leaves, and seeds of this plant have been traditionally used in various cultures for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Melia azedarach var. intermedia include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural insect repellent. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
